Title: Genome Assembly Revealed MdZAT5 Coordinates Anthocyanin Biosynthesis in Apple Fruit Peel and Flesh by Interacting With MdHY5

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit peel; fruit flesh; apple calli
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases anthocyanin accumulation, whereas knockdown/silencing reduces pigmentation and anthocyanin content.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator coordinating anthocyanin biosynthesis in both peel and flesh; light- and MeJA-responsive activator.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of MdCHS, MdCHI, and MdF3H; enhances transcription of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via direct promoter binding; activity enhanced by protein interaction with MdHY5
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a transcriptional complex with MdHY5
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA/RNA-seq candidate identification; RT-qPCR; transient overexpression in apple fruit; VIGS/RNAi knockdown; stable transformation in apple calli; anthocyanin quantification; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay; GUS transactivation assay; Y2H; pull-down; BiFC; luciferase complementation imaging
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdZAT5-3G was identified as a hub transcription factor correlated with anthocyanin accumulation in both peel and flesh. 2. MdZAT5-3G is induced by light and MeJA and directly binds the promoters of MdCHS, MdCHI, and MdF3H. 3. Overexpression of MdZAT5-3G promotes anthocyanin accumulation in fruit and calli, while suppression reduces pigmentation. 4. MdZAT5-3G interacts with MdHY5, which enhances its promoter activation activity.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light treatment; MeJA treatment; fruit ripening stage; high light in calli
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for breeding or engineering red-fleshed/red-peeled apples with enhanced anthocyanin content; may be stacked with MdHY5 or other anthocyanin regulators.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive, experimental overexpression); native MdCHS/MdCHI/MdF3H promoters assayed as targets
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in fruit, especially during ripening, in both peel and flesh; induced in calli by light and MeJA.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; NCBI BioProject PRJNA1288137; PRJNA1288132; PRJNA546101; PRJNA546107
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Two chromosome-3 copies in the two haplotypes have identical sequences and are functionally active; structural predictions suggest distinct binding pocket architecture relative to chromosome-11 copies, potentially underlying functional divergence.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances anthocyanin accumulation, particularly by cooperating with MdZAT5-3G.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive co-regulator required to maximize MdZAT5-3G-mediated anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances MdZAT5-3G-mediated activation of MdCHS, MdCHI, and MdF3H promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MdCHS; MdCHI; MdF3H (via cooperation with MdZAT5-3G)
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: light in fruit peel; MeJA in calli/flesh context
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a transcriptional complex with MdZAT5-3G
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression profiling; transient co-overexpression and silencing in apple fruit; anthocyanin measurement; RT-qPCR; Y2H; pull-down; BiFC; luciferase complementation imaging; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdHY5 shows expression patterns similar to MdZAT5-3G during fruit development. 2. MdHY5 physically interacts with MdZAT5-3G in yeast, in vitro, and in planta. 3. Co-expression of MdHY5 with MdZAT5-3G increases anthocyanin accumulation and target gene expression beyond either factor alone. 4. Silencing MdHY5 suppresses MdZAT5-3G-driven anthocyanin accumulation, indicating functional dependence.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful co-engineering target with MdZAT5-3G to strengthen anthocyanin pigmentation in apple tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by light in peel; induced by MeJA in calli; expression pattern parallels MdZAT5-3G during fruit development.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; NCBI BioProject PRJNA1288137; PRJNA1288132; PRJNA546101; PRJNA546107
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this paper MdHY5 is functionally characterized mainly as an interacting cofactor that enhances MdZAT5-3G DNA binding/transactivation rather than as the primary discovery target.
